zh.Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England: 英國巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Richard Baldwin於1415年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，他的父親Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury25歲，母親Lady Mary Astell15歲。他於1440年與Agnes Augustina Dormer在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1466年在英國巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓享年51歲，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓聖 邁克爾 安德 哥吉拉 安爾也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Richard Baldwin于1415年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾时，他的父亲Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury25岁，母亲Lady Mary Astell15岁。他于1440年与Agnes Augustina Dormer在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1466年在英国巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓享年51岁，埋葬在英国巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓聖 邁克爾 安德 哥吉拉 安爾也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Baldwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’, which was extremely popular among the Normans and in Flanders in the early Middle Ages. It was the personal name of the Crusader who in 1100 became the first Christian king of Jerusalem, and of four more Crusader kings of Jerusalem. It was also borne by Baldwin, Count of Flanders (1172–1205), leader of the Fourth Crusade, who became first Latin Emperor of Constantinople (1204). In North America, this surname has absorbed Dutch forms such as Boudewijn.
Irish: surname adopted in Donegal by bearers of the Gaelic surname Ó Maolagáin (see Milligan ), due to association of Gaelic maol ‘bald, hairless’ with English bald.
History: A John Baldwin from Buckinghamshire, England, arrived in the US in 1638 and settled in Milford, CT.

variants.name: Milligan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolagáin ‘descendant of Maoileagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of maol meaning ‘bald’ or ‘tonsured’.

variants.name: Baldovino
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Philippines and Colombia) and Italian: from the personal name Baldovino, from ancient Germanic Baldowin (see Baldwin ).

variants.name: Bellmann
meanings: North German:
habitational name for someone from Belle in Westphalia, Bell in the Rhineland, or Bellen near Bremen.
in Saxony, from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal name   Baldwin  .

variants.name: Bais
meanings: Dutch (mainly North Holland): patronymic from the personal name Baije, which is a derivative of an ancient Germanic name beginning with the element bern- ‘bear’, or possibly a variant of Boije, which may be a derivative of Boidin and thus a variant of Baldwin .
Italian (Friuli Venezia Giulia and Trentino-South Tyrol): nickname derived from German weiss ‘white’.

Americanized form of Croatian and Slovenian Bajs: nickname from dialect bajs ‘bass’ (the musical instrument), figuratively ‘fatty, potbelly’. The surname in the form Bais is found in Croatia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Baudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Etienne, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Firmin, Francoise, Jean-Yves, Laurent, Minos, Sylvain.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Boudoin 1 and Bowdoin .
Altered form of French   Beaudoin  , a cognate (variant) of 1 above.

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Balkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Herschel.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish female personal name Beyle meaning ‘beautiful’ (related to French belle).

English (of Norman origin): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Baldekin, Baudekin, an Anglo-Norman borrowing of Middle Dutch Baudequin, a Flemish pet form of Baldwin . However, a clear link between Middle English Baudekin and post-medieval Balkin has not been established, and the modern name may have a different source, a variant of Balcom perhaps, by migration.

variants.name: Bawden
meanings: English: from a late variant of the Norman personal name   Baldwin   or a variant of   Bowden  .

variants.name: Baldon
meanings: English: habitational name from either Marsh Baldon or Toot Baldon in Oxfordshire, from the Old English personal name Bealda + dūn ‘hill’, or a variant of Baldwin . Occasionally the name may be from Baildon in Yorkshire (named with Old English bēgel ‘bend’ + dūn ‘hill’; recorded as Baldon in the 16th century), or from Ballidon in Derbyshire (named with Old English belg ‘bag, sack’ + denu ‘valley’).
English: variant of   Boldon  .
Italian (Veneto): from an augmentative of the personal name   Baldo  .

variants.name: Beaudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Serge, Jacques, Gilles, Laurent, Normand, Pierre, Alcide, Lucien, Raoul.

French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Baudoin , Baudouin , Beaudion , Boadway , Boardway , Bodeau , Bodoh , Bodway , Boudway , and Budway .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Richard Baldwin was born in 1415, in Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury, was 25 and his mother, Lady Mary Astell, was 15. He married Agnes Augustina Dormer in 1440, in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1466, in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 51, and was buried in St Michael and All Angels' Church, Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England.

Il a épousé Agnes Augustina Dormer en 1440, à Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1466, à Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à St Michael and All Angels' Church, Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Richard Baldwinが1415年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesburyは25歳，母Lady Mary Astellは15歳であった。1440年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリーでAgnes Augustina Dormerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1466年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアアストン クリントンで51歳で死去し，イギリスブクインガムシュアイアアストン クリントンスト ミチャエル アンド アル アンジェルス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Michael and All Angels Church, Ast: 영국 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴 스트 믹하얼 앤드 앨 앤그스' c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Richard Baldwin(이)가 1415년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Mary Astell(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1440년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이에서 Agnes Augustina Dormer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1466년 영국 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴 스트 믹하얼 앤드 앨 앤그스' c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Agnes Augustina Dormer em 1440, em Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.Saint Michael and All Angels Church, Ast: ст мачаел анд алл ангелс' чурч, астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Richard Baldwin родился в 1415 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Mary Astell, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Augustina Dormer в 1440 г. в местности аилесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1466 г. в местности астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности ст мачаел анд алл ангелс' чурч, астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия.
